title: ProgramReferral
description: A referral connecting a person or court case to a MOCJ-coordinated program (e.g. Supervised Release, an alternative-to-incarceration, reentry, or mental-health diversion program). NET-NEW: MOCJ is a coordination office whose whole purpose is to route people between courts, agencies, and community providers along the Sequential Intercept Model — yet there is NO public API and NO Open Data twin for a referral. Today referrals happen offline (court mandate, case manager, phone) or via generic Contact Form 7 web forms. This schema serves both the read surface (list/get a referral's status) and the ProgramReferralRequest write surface (make a referral). NOTE: a real deployment must treat referral data as sensitive (justice-involved individuals) and gate it behind authentication and consent.

referralId.description: Server-assigned referral identifier. Omit on create; returned on the created resource.

status.description: Referral lifecycle status. Set by MOCJ / the provider, not the referrer.

docketId.description: Associated court docket, if the referral stems from a case (ties to SupervisedReleaseDocket).

needsSummary.description: Free-text summary of the need / reason for referral (avoid unnecessary personal identifiers).

urgent.description: Flag time-sensitive referrals (e.g. imminent court date, safety concern).

consentGiven.description: Whether the individual consented to the referral and to sharing their information with the provider.

contact.description: How the provider or MOCJ can follow up with the referrer or, with consent, the individual.
